steps.title: Identify what the blank must express
steps.content:
The sentence says the speed difference is “largely ______ of the fact that flight features limit top speeds less than swimming features.” The blank follows a cause-and-effect structure: the fact about features explains the speed gap. The blank must name the relationship between the speed difference and its cause.
  • Choice A — a consequence: Means a result produced by a cause — exactly describes the speed gap as an outcome of the feature difference. ✓
  • Choice B — an explanation: An explanation is something that makes something else understandable; the speed difference doesn’t explain the feature difference — it’s the other way around. ✗
  • Choice C — a repudiation: Means a rejection or denial; the sentence contains no denial. ✗
  • Choice D — an objective: Means a goal; the speed difference is not a goal. ✗

steps.title: Confirm and conclude
steps.content: Only “a consequence” correctly captures that the speed difference results from the way flight and swimming features limit top speeds differently. That confirms A is correct.
